archetype: High-Voltage Circuit Breaker Arc Flash & Sequence-Of-Events Recorder
description: A deterministic, hard-real-time system that parses IEC 61850 GOOSE/SV streams from digital relays to detect arc flash events within 4 milliseconds and trigger physical tripping mechanisms. It serves grid protection engineers and utility operators, requiring nanosecond-precision timestamp synchronization across distributed hardware to reconstruct fault sequences in high-voltage environments.
rules: MUST implement IEEE 1588-2008 PTP (Precision Time Protocol) with hardware timestamping to ensure sub-microsecond clock synchronization across all substation devices.
MUST process IEC 61850-8-1 GOOSE messages with deterministic jitter under 50 microseconds to guarantee protection relay coordination.
MUST support IEC 60255 standards for functional safety (SIL 2/3) regarding failure detection and safe shutdown procedures.
MUST maintain a write-ahead log (WAL) on non-volatile memory that survives total power loss to preserve the exact sequence of events during catastrophic grid failures.
MUST enforce strict memory partitioning to prevent any user-space application from blocking the real-time protection kernel.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use garbage-collected languages or dynamic memory allocation during the fault detection loop, as GC pauses could delay tripping signals and cause equipment destruction.
DO NOT rely on standard TCP/IP networking stacks for critical trip signals; use connectionless UDP or dedicated fiber-optic point-to-point links to avoid retransmission latency.

DO NOT aggregate logging data in real-time for analysis; raw waveform data (COMTRADE) must be buffered locally before any remote transmission to prevent network congestion from masking critical fault signatures.
key_technical_challenges: Synchronizing distributed sampling across geographically separated bays with <1 microsecond skew to accurately calculate differential protection currents.
Implementing hardware-accelerated digital signal processing (DSP) for FFT analysis of transient waveforms within a 2ms execution window.
Ensuring cyber-physical security where encryption overhead does not exceed the strict timing budgets required for emergency grid isolation.

recommended_stack.language: C17 with strict static analysis constraints
recommended_stack.framework: RT-Kernel (e.g., RT-Linux with PREEMPT_RT patch or VxWorks)
recommended_stack.database_engine: Time-Series DB with WAL (e.g., TimescaleDB on a dedicated read-only replica for post-mortem)
recommended_stack.cache: Hardware-managed L1/L2 cache with locked memory regions
recommended_stack.message_broker: ZeroMQ with in-process bindings for sub-millisecond IPC
special_services: GOOSE Message Parser with ASN.1 BER decoding
